bins.name: Study SLA and fast track
bins.desc: Paces supports expedited treatment and recommends a mandatory SLA of under 20 business days for initial studies. It argues that AI automation is the only way to meet such a timeline at scale, and that the fastest interconnection track should be reserved for dispatchable loads sited at power-ready buses.
quotes: interconnection studies up to 20x faster with full audit trails and sign-off

State that transmission providers are encouraged to use AI agents to operate approved study tools and scripts, as long as qualified engineers own the methodology and sign-off and workflows are auditable.
Encourage or require a SLA of <20 business days for all large load initial studies which makes the leveraging of AI automation essential to meet such a timeline.
sited at buses with positive headroom and nearby developable land
Permit slower timelines and clearer upgrade expectations for heavily constrained, non-power-ready locations.

bins.name: Upgrade expectations at constrained sites
bins.desc: Paces supports giving constrained, non-power-ready locations slower timelines paired with clearer upgrade cost expectations, so that speculative projects at costly sites face transparent signals rather than joining the same queue as well-sited loads.
quotes: Permit slower timelines and clearer upgrade expectations for heavily constrained, non-power-ready locations.

bins.name: AI-automated interconnection studies
bins.desc: Paces argues that manual study processes cannot scale to meet large-load demand and endorses AI agents running approved power-flow tools under engineer oversight. It recommends FERC explicitly permit this approach and pair it with a short study SLA to make automation necessary.
quotes: interconnection studies up to 20x faster with full audit trails and sign-off

manual study processes and speculative, project-by-project signals are producing slow interconnection queues and plans for load that will never be built, while a small set of constraints strands the load that actually can be built
FERC's large-loads rule is the chance to fix both problems

State that transmission providers are encouraged to use AI agents to operate approved study tools and scripts, as long as qualified engineers own the methodology and sign-off and workflows are auditable.
Encourage or require a SLA of <20 business days for all large load initial studies which makes the leveraging of AI automation essential to meet such a timeline.

Direct transmission providers that face material large-load growth to build parcel-level maps of developable large-load sites (zoning, setbacks, floodplains, water, ownership, etc.) and overlay them with bus-level withdrawal headroom.
the real operational risk is mis-located, speculative load plus manual-only study processes that cannot scale

bins.name: Dispatchability as fast-track condition
bins.desc: Paces supports tying the fastest interconnection track to loads that are dispatchable or curtailable, treating flexibility as a prerequisite for expedited treatment rather than an optional feature.
quotes: sited at buses with positive headroom and nearby developable land

bins.name: Land-aware, constraint-aware siting
bins.desc: Paces argues that speculative, poorly sited load produces grid plans that do not match where load will actually be built. It recommends requiring parcel-level developability maps overlaid with bus-level headroom so planning and interconnection decisions reflect actual siting constraints.
quotes: manual study processes and speculative, project-by-project signals are producing slow interconnection queues and plans for load that will never be built, while a small set of constraints strands the load that actually can be built

Direct transmission providers that face material large-load growth to build parcel-level maps of developable large-load sites (zoning, setbacks, floodplains, water, ownership, etc.) and overlay them with bus-level withdrawal headroom.
sited at buses with positive headroom and nearby developable land
Permit slower timelines and clearer upgrade expectations for heavily constrained, non-power-ready locations.
the real operational risk is mis-located, speculative load plus manual-only study processes that cannot scale

bins.name: FERC endorsement of AI agents
bins.desc: Paces asks FERC to formally endorse AI agent automation inside existing reliability frameworks, provided engineers retain sign-off authority and workflows produce auditable outputs. It also requests a technical conference where it can demonstrate real-world examples.
quotes: State that transmission providers are encouraged to use AI agents to operate approved study tools and scripts, as long as qualified engineers own the methodology and sign-off and workflows are auditable.
Encourage or require a SLA of <20 business days for all large load initial studies which makes the leveraging of AI automation essential to meet such a timeline.

bins.name: Parcel-level planning maps
bins.desc: Paces recommends requiring transmission providers facing material large-load growth to build parcel-level maps covering zoning, setbacks, floodplains, water access, and ownership, then overlay those maps with bus-level withdrawal headroom to guide both planning and siting decisions.
quotes: Direct transmission providers that face material large-load growth to build parcel-level maps of developable large-load sites (zoning, setbacks, floodplains, water, ownership, etc.) and overlay them with bus-level withdrawal headroom.
sited at buses with positive headroom and nearby developable land
